title: Did Not.
content:
'Twas a new feeling--something more
    Than we had dared to own before.
        Which then we hid not;
    We saw it in each other's eye,
    And wished, in every half-breathed sigh,
        To speak, but did not.

    She felt my lips' impassioned touch--
    'Twas the first time I dared so much,
        And yet she chid not;
    But whispered o'er my burning brow,
    "Oh! do you doubt I love you now?"
        Sweet soul! I did not.

    Warmly I felt her bosom thrill,
    I prest it closer, closer still,
        Though gently bid not;
    Till--oh! the world hath seldom heard
    Of lovers, who so nearly erred,
        And yet, who did not.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "Did Not.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
